scenario: An ops engineer finds 400 stuck instances of an old process version, all with Incidents on the same task. The fix is in v2; the team wants to migrate all 400 at once rather than per-instance clicks.
question: Which Operate feature fits?

options.text: Process only one instance at a time in Operate, requiring 400 individual clicks to handle all stuck instances. Operate provides batch operations specifically to avoid this. Using per-instance actions when a large number of instances require the same operation is highly inefficient. The Selection Operations feature allows filtering a set of instances and applying a batch action such as cancel, retry, modify, or migrate to all matching instances simultaneously.

Batch operations in Operate -- select all 400 stuck instances via the filter panel, then apply the batch action in one shot. For migrating instances to a fixed process version, use Process Instance Batch Modification to move tokens to the corrected flow node across all selected instances simultaneously. Batch operations eliminate the need for per-instance actions when many instances require the same treatment.

Write a script using the Zeebe gRPC or Orchestration Cluster REST API that loops over all 400 instance IDs and triggers migration or modification on each one. While scripting is technically feasible for large-scale batch operations, Operate's built-in batch modification UI provides the same outcome without requiring custom code. API scripting is more appropriate for automated pipelines rather than ad-hoc operational recovery.

Delete the old BPMN process definition from the cluster using the undeployment API. Deleting a process definition removes it from the registry but does not affect running instances that are already using it -- those instances continue executing against the old version until they complete or are migrated. Deleting the definition would not fix the stuck instances; it would only prevent new instances from being created with that version.

a.text: Incorrect. Operate supports batch operations on selected/filtered instance sets -- cancel, retry, modify, migrate. Cuts 400 clicks to one batch action.

c.text: Incorrect. UI batch is faster for ad-hoc work.

d.text: Incorrect. Deleting BPMN doesn't fix stuck instances.
